preview_text:
The model favours Czech Republic at 54% to win, while South Africa's probability stands at 17%. The underdog faces a substantial gap, though the inclusion of the draw—at 29%—softens the binary contest and suggests the match retains competitive uncertainty.

The win-draw-loss distribution indicates a moderately open fixture. Czech Republic's advantage is clear but not overwhelming; the draw probability near 29% reflects a plausible outcome in which neither side dominates. The 37-percentage-point spread between the favourite and underdog points to a tilted but not one-sided encounter.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge noticeably. The model rates Czech Republic 5–6 percentage points higher than either the bookmaker consensus or Polymarket, while assessing South Africa's chances 5–6 points lower than both. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the variance may reflect differing methodologies or information sets.
